description_long: This object is a historical artillery cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark, weathered metal, featuring ornate engravings and a reinforced muzzle. The carriage is constructed from heavy timber, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and shows signs of age and wear. Two large metal rings are attached to the top of the barrel for maneuvering or lifting. The wheels are solid and robust, designed to support the heavy weight of the cannon and allow limited movement. The overall appearance suggests a piece of military equipment from the pre-modern era, likely used for siege or defensive purposes.
description: A large, ornate metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four solid wheels and metal reinforcements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the detailed engravings, and the wooden carriage structure.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back end of the carriage, with the wheels visible on either side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the length of the barrel, carriage details, and the metal rings on top.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed large-caliber cannon barrel mounted centrally on a wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the structure consists of exposed metal and wood with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the surface is a solid, weathered dark green and brown with no distinct camouflage pattern.
